doc_title: IP 69012, "Class I Research and Test Reactors Radiation Protection."

steps.text: Verify inspection requirements using direct observation, records review, independent measurements, or discussions with licensee personnel.
Review the changes to the facility's radiation protection procedures.
Directly observe the postings of Form NRC-3 in one or two areas.
Observe the controls for two radiologically controlled areas.
Evaluate the licensee's use of controls for radiation areas unique to Research and Test reactors.
Evaluate the licensee's use of shielding and controls used to remove or modify shielding.
Observe the protective clothing worn by two facility experimenters or other personnel during work activities, or conduct discussions if observation is not practical.
Observe the personnel dosimetry worn by four facility workers, including one operator and one experimenter.
Observe two selected facility workers leaving the applicable controlled area to determine if dosimeter readings are recorded.
Review the badge processing setup if the licensee processes its own film or TLD badges.
Observe four facility workers leaving a controlled area to check exit survey compliance.
Evaluate the radiation safety training received by two permanent facility workers, and where applicable, two experimenters or students.
Observe respective activities and discuss the radiation safety training received by these individuals.
Observe two radiological surveys, sampling, or monitoring activities by accompanying a qualified person or requesting a demonstration.
Determine whether radiation levels are above those specified in 10 CFR Part 20, Subpart D.
Perform independent radiological surveys as needed to verify licensee assumptions or measurements.
Compare independent measurements with those completed by the licensee in the same areas.
Review surveys conducted by the licensee during both shutdown and operations following changes in shielding.
Examine surveys of solid waste material to determine compliance.
Verify the calibration of radiation monitoring instrument(s) used in the licensee's radiation surveys.
Examine the technical adequacy of calibration procedures at facilities that perform their own calibrations.
Review 50 percent of the facility's personnel dosimetry records.
Determine if the licensee reported overexposures and inform appropriate management and the NRR project manager.
Review major changes in organization, personnel, facilities, equipment, programs, and procedures since the last inspection.
Review major radiation control events at the facility since the last inspection.
Inform appropriate management and the NRR project manager if radiologically significant events were not effectively addressed.
Verify ALARA implementation for one routinely performed experiment or process with high radiological exposure potential.
Review and audit the radiation protection program to assess compliance, performance, and effectiveness.

preconditions.text: Before any regulatory action is taken, the inspector is to consult with responsible management and the NRR project manager.
If postings of Form NRC-3 are not readily apparent.
If licensee procedures require that personnel exposures on direct reading dosimeters be recorded.
If the licensee processes its own film or TLD badges.
If radiation levels above background are suspected in areas outside of the restricted area.
If there were exposures greater than the 10 CFR Part 20 limits.
If radiologically significant events were not effectively addressed by the licensee.

constraints.text: Do not enter a high radiation area without a licensee escort trained in radiation protection who is knowledgeable of the area and precautions.
This inspection procedure is not to be used for the inspection of material licenses of the NRC or Agreement State licenses.
